subtitle: An old lion’s march back to his lair

panels.shot: low-angle mid-wide of an aged warrior on horseback against a dusky Bihar sky
panels.action: Kunwar Singh, grey-bearded yet unbowed, sits upright on a sturdy horse on a dusty country road near Jagdispur, one hand on his sword-hilt, monsoon clouds massing above the flat fields behind him.

dialogue.text: Kunwar Singh of Jagdispur, in the rising of 1857, marched homeward in his eighties to test if an old arm could yet strike for land and honour.

i18n.hi: १८५७ की ज्वाला में, जगदीसपुर के कुँवर सिंह अस्सी पार उम्र में घर लौटे—देखने, बूढ़ी बाँह अभी ज़मीन और इज़्जत के लिए चल सकती है या नहीं।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of the Arrah countryside with British entrenchment in the distance
panels.action: From a gentle rise, Kunwar Singh stands with a small escort, watching the distant outline of the Arrah House entrenchment where British officers and sepoys once held out amid the fields and groves of Shahabad.

dialogue.text: The district of Shahabad burned early in 1857. Sepoys rose, talukdars armed, and at Arrah a small British garrison clung to its brick house while the countryside slipped from Company hands.

i18n.hi: १८५७ में शाहाबाद ज़िला जल्दी भड़क उठा। सिपाही उठ खड़े हुए, तालुक़दारों ने हथियार उठाए, और आरा में एक छोटा अंग्रेज़ी दस्ता ईंट के घर से चिपका रहा, जबकि देहात कंपनी के हाथों से फिसल गया।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh in his fort courtyard, surrounded by retainers
panels.action: In the old fort of Jagdispur, Kunwar Singh, wrapped in a simple angarkha and turban, leans on his staff as armed retainers and village elders argue around him under the shade of a neem tree.

dialogue.text: Kunwar Singh, zamindar of Jagdispur, already long at odds with the Company over revenue and authority, found his grievances suddenly carried on the wider storm of the Bengal Army’s mutiny.

i18n.hi: जगदीसपुर के ज़मींदार कुँवर सिंह की कंपनी से लगान और हक़ पर पुरानी ठनी हुई थी; अब उनकी शिकायतें बंगाल सेना की बग़ावत की आँधी पर सवार हो गईं।

panels.shot: close-up on Kunwar Singh’s lined face, lit by lamplight
panels.action: By lamplight in an inner chamber, Kunwar Singh sits cross-legged, maps and farm-accounts spread beside him, his face creased in thought as he weighs the future of his estate and people.

dialogue.text: He was no hothead youth. He had lived through treaties, revenue demands, and the slow bite of loss. Now, at more than eighty, he chose to risk what remained for a cause larger than his fields.

i18n.hi: वो कोई जोशीला लड़का नहीं थे। उन्होंने फ़रमान, लगान की माँगें और धीरे–धीरे होती ज़मीन की काट सब देखी थी। अब, अस्सी के पार, उन्होंने अपनी बची ज़िंदगी भी खेत से बड़ी लड़ाई पर दाँव पर लगा दी।

panels.shot: dynamic mid-wide of Kunwar Singh on horseback leading a mixed force
panels.action: Kunwar Singh rides at the head of a column of sepoys, retainers, and villagers along a dusty track, their muskets and matchlocks slung, as they move away from Jagdispur into the wider conflict.

dialogue.text: Through 1857 and early 1858, his banner moved with the mutineers. He fought near Arrah, shifted with the broken regiments towards the Ganga, striking where the Company seemed weakest.

i18n.hi: १८५७ से १८५८ की शुरुआत तक उनका निशान बाग़ी सिपाहियों के साथ चला। उन्होंने आरा के पास लड़ा, टूटी फौजों के साथ गंगा की ओर खिसके, जहाँ कंपनी कमज़ोर लगी, वहीं वार किया।

panels.shot: bird’s-eye over the Ganga ghats with marching columns and boats
panels.action: On the broad banks of the Ganga, Kunwar Singh’s weary men file towards boats under a hazy sky, while across the river British-led columns and guns can be seen in the distance beginning to assemble.

dialogue.text: But the Company was not idle. Reinforcements came, columns pushed up from the east and west, and the river that had long fed Bihar now carried gunboats and discipline back into the plains.

i18n.hi: पर कंपनी भी खाली नहीं बैठी। मदद पहुँची, पूरब–पश्चिम से कॉलम चढ़ आए, और जो गंगा सदियों से बिहार को पालती थी, वही अब तोपों और अनुशासन से भरी नावें लेकर मैदानी इलाक़ों में लौटी।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh conferring with lieutenants in a camp at dusk
panels.action: In a rough riverside camp at dusk, Kunwar Singh, fatigue etched in his posture, sits on a low charpoy, conferring with a few officers as tired sepoys cook near small fires in the background.

dialogue.text: By the spring of 1858 his bands had marched hard and fought often. Men were scattered, supplies thin, and around them districts fell one by one back under British control.

i18n.hi: १८५८ की बहार तक उनके दस्ते लगातार चले, लगातार लड़े। लोग बिखर गए, रसद पतली पड़ गई, और चारों ओर के ज़िले एक–एक कर फिर से अंग्रेज़ी क़ब्ज़े में चले गए।

panels.shot: close-up of Kunwar Singh’s hands over a rough map, with Jagdispur marked
panels.action: On a rolled-out, stained map, Kunwar Singh’s weathered hand rests firmly over the name ‘Jagdispur’ as his other hand gestures, indicating routes through villages and fields back towards his home estate.

dialogue.text: In that tightening ring, he made a hard, simple choice. Rather than wander with broken columns, he would turn back to Jagdispur and try to raise his own soil once more.

i18n.hi: उस घिरते घेरे में उन्होंने सादा, सख़्त फ़ैसला किया। टूटी–फूटी फौजों के साथ भटकने से बेहतर, वे जगदीसपुर लौटेंगे और अपनी मिट्टी को फिर एक बार जगा कर देखेंगे।

panels.shot: long shot of a small, determined column marching through fields and mango groves
panels.action: A reduced but disciplined band marches behind Kunwar Singh along a narrow path through ripening fields and mango groves, villagers watching from a distance as dust rises around the returning leader.

dialogue.text: So, with a smaller following, he moved westward and north, slipping between stronger British forces, avoiding open battle, keeping his eyes fixed always on the line of trees that marked Jagdispur.

i18n.hi: तो कम लोगों के साथ, वे पश्चिम–उत्तर को मुड़े, मज़बूत अंग्रेज़ी फौजों के बीच से फिसलते हुए, खुली जंग से बचते, नज़र हमेशा उन पेड़ों की रेखा पर रखी रही, जहाँ से जगदीसपुर शुरू होता था।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh speaking to a young officer while marching
panels.action: As they march, Kunwar Singh rides close beside a younger officer on foot, leaning down slightly, his expression calm but resolute while the younger man listens intently amid the tramp of feet.

dialogue.speaker: Kunwar Singh
dialogue.text: Do not chase glory on the road, beta. Hold your strength for home.

i18n.hi: राह में शोहरत मत ढूँढ, बेटा। ताक़त घर के लिए बचा कर रख।

panels.shot: medium shot by a riverside path with distant British patrol faintly visible
panels.action: On a narrow riverside path, Kunwar Singh reins in his horse, half-turned towards faint figures of a distant British patrol across the water, while his men crouch among reeds, waiting in tense silence.

dialogue.text: British reports later traced his movements with unease. Columns hunted him, but an old zamindar who knew every canal, grove, and village track in Shahabad was not easily pinned.

i18n.hi: अँग्रेज़ी रिपोर्टों ने बाद में उनकी चालों को घबराहट के साथ दर्ज किया। कॉलम उनका शिकार करने निकले, पर शाहाबाद की हर नहर, बग़ीचा और पगडंडी जानने वाला बूढ़ा ज़मींदार आसानी से फँसने वाला नहीं था।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh’s arm bound with cloth, resting yet composed
panels.action: In a shaded halt beneath a banyan, Kunwar Singh sits while a companion wraps his forearm with cloth; he rests quietly, back straight, surrounded by a few worried followers and stacked muskets.

dialogue.text: Tradition would later embroider this march with stories of self-mutilation and sacrifice. The records speak more plainly of hardship, wounds, and an old man’s stubborn refusal to stop.

i18n.hi: रिवायतों ने बाद में इस सफ़र के साथ देह–काट और बलिदान की कहानियाँ जोड़ दीं। दस्तावेज़ बस सीधी बात करते हैं—कठिनाई, ज़ख़्म, और एक बूढ़े की ज़िद कि वो रुकने वाला नहीं।

panels.shot: wide shot of British-led troops advancing toward Jagdispur from another direction
panels.action: On another road lined with palms, a disciplined British-led column with Indian troops, artillery in tow, advances towards Jagdispur, officers pointing ahead while camp-followers trail in the heat shimmer.

dialogue.text: Meanwhile, British officers, writing from Patna and Arrah, noted with alarm that Kunwar Singh aimed again at his estate. They moved troops to forestall him and to stamp out any new rising there.

i18n.hi: उधर पटना और आरा से लिखते अंग्रेज़ अफ़सर घबराए नोट भेज रहे थे—कुँवर सिंह फिर अपने जायदाद की तरफ़ बढ़ रहे हैं। उन्होंने आगे बढ़कर उन्हें रोकने और वहाँ किसी नई बग़ावत की चिंगारी दबाने को फौजें मोड़ दीं।

panels.shot: dramatic wide of Jagdispur’s outskirts as Kunwar Singh’s force approaches at dawn
panels.action: At pale dawn, Kunwar Singh and his men crest a low ridge to see the outlines of Jagdispur—its fort walls, trees, and scattered houses—emerging from the morning mist beyond the fields.

dialogue.text: Yet in April 1858, after months of marching and fighting, he came within sight of Jagdispur again. The returning column saw not only home, but a field where the revolt might blaze anew.

i18n.hi: फिर भी, अप्रैल १८५८ में, महीनों की चाल और लड़ाइयों के बाद, वे फिर जगदीसपुर की हद में दिख पड़े। लौटती टोली ने सिर्फ़ घर नहीं देखा, एक ऐसा मैदान भी देखा जहाँ बग़ावत फिर भभक सकती थी।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh addressing his men before entering Jagdispur
panels.action: Near a tree-lined lane leading towards the town, Kunwar Singh dismounts, standing firmly as his men gather close, their faces expectant, while the skyline of Jagdispur rises behind them.

dialogue.speaker: Kunwar Singh
dialogue.text: We have wandered far. Today, we stand for our own soil and name.

i18n.hi: हम बहुत भटके। आज, अपनी मिट्टी और अपना नाम बचाने खड़े हैं।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of Kunwar Singh walking through Jagdispur’s gate with followers
panels.action: From a low angle, Kunwar Singh strides through the old gateway of Jagdispur, flanked by armed followers, as townspeople peer from doorways and rooftops, some folding hands in silent respect.

dialogue.text: Contemporary records agree that he re-entered his estate and rallied men there, recovering Jagdispur even as British forces circled, astonished that a leader of his years still seized the initiative.

i18n.hi: उस दौर के रिकॉर्ड एक बात पर मिलते हैं—उन्होंने अपनी जायदाद में फिर क़दम रखा, वहीं लोगों को जुटाया, और अंग्रेज़ी घेराबंदी के बीच जगदीसपुर वापस ले लिया; अफ़सर हैरान थे कि इतने बूढ़े नेता ने भी पहल छीन ली।

panels.shot: wide shot of the environs near Jagdispur prepared for defense
panels.action: Outside the town, village paths are blocked with felled trees and earthen mounds; Kunwar Singh stands on a rise, overseeing the placement of men along bunds and groves as they ready for attack.

dialogue.text: He knew he could not match the Company’s numbers or guns. Instead he trusted his ground—canals, groves, and bunds—and the spirit of men fighting within sight of their own homes.

i18n.hi: उन्हें पता था कि वह कंपनी की गिनती या तोप से टक्कर नहीं दे सकते। उन्होंने भरोसा रखा अपने भूगोल पर—नहरों, बग़ीचों, बाँधों पर—और उन मर्दों की रूह पर जो अपने ही घरों के सामने लड़ रहे थे।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kunwar Singh watching distant smoke on the horizon
panels.action: Kunwar Singh stands on the parapet of a wall, gripping it lightly, as faint smoke columns rise on the horizon where British troops and his own men are clashing beyond the visible frame.

dialogue.text: Clashes followed. British chroniclers, like Forrest and Tayler, wrote with reluctance of setbacks and stubborn resistance around Jagdispur, admitting that an aged chief had yet managed to sting them.

i18n.hi: मुठभेड़ें लगी रहीं। फॉरेस्ट और टेलर जैसे अंग्रेज़ इतिहासकारों ने भी अनिच्छा से लिखा—जगदीसपुर के आस–पास उन्हें मार और ज़िद्दी प्रतिरोध झेलना पड़ा; मानना पड़ा कि एक बूढ़े सरदार ने उन्हें फिर भी डँसा दिया।

panels.shot: intimate close-up of Kunwar Singh seated inside his fort, eyes half-closed
panels.action: Within a quiet chamber of the fort, Kunwar Singh sits on a low seat, eyes half-closed, one hand resting on his knee, as distant sounds of movement echo faintly through the stone corridors.

dialogue.text: Illness and age soon pressed harder than any column. Within days of his triumph, Kunwar Singh’s long life, woven through Mughal decline and British rise, bent toward its end in his own stronghold.

i18n.hi: बीमारी और उम्र ने जल्दी ही हर कॉलम से ज़्यादा दबाव डाला। जीत के कुछ ही दिन बाद, मुग़ल ढलान और अंग्रेज़ उभार के पूरे दौर को जी चुके कुँवर सिंह की लंबी उम्र अपने ही गढ़ में आख़िर मोड़ पर झुक गई।

panels.shot: wide, contemplative shot of Jagdispur’s landscape with fields and fort in evening light
panels.action: In the soft light of evening, the fields around Jagdispur stretch calm and green, the fort’s outline standing sober against the sky, while small figures of farmers move along the bunds.

dialogue.text: The revolt of 1857 was crushed, yet in Jagdispur the memory of an octogenarian landlord who marched back through war to defend his estate endured, a quiet lesson in courage and rooted resolve.

i18n.hi: १८५७ की बग़ावत कुचल दी गई, पर जगदीसपुर में उस अस्सी पार ज़मींदार की याद रही, जो जंग से गुज़र कर अपनी जायदाद बचाने लौटा—हिम्मत और जड़ पकड़ी हुई नीयत का चुपचाप सबक।

subtitle_i18n.en: An old lion’s march back to his lair
subtitle_i18n.hi: बूढ़े शेर की वापसी अपनी माँद में
